AcceptanceLedgerSummary.description:
WOG1-F3 marked-provisional bridge: the ON-DISK
`acceptance_ledger.json` (written by
`acceptance.acceptance_ledger_bytes`) is a DIFFERENT shape than the
in-memory `regolith.orchestrator.acceptance.AcceptanceOutcome`
model it is rendered from (no `accepted_hashes` field on disk, and
`accepted_deviations` replaces `deviations`) -- there is no
regolith model that round-trips this file directly, so
`AcceptanceOutcome.model_validate_json` fails against it. This is a
graphite-local shape mirroring exactly what ships to disk, escalated
to the lithos coordinator as a gap (a `LedgerDocument` model in
`regolith.orchestrator.acceptance` would close it).
AcceptanceLedgerSummary.title: AcceptanceLedgerSummary

AcceptanceOutcome.description:
The release gate's read of the ledger (WO-98).

``accepted_hashes`` are the obligation content hashes an
evidence-carrying, trust-floor-meeting, unexpired waiver accepted --
the gate removes them from the refusing counts (kept DISTINCT as
``GateCounts.accepted_deviation``, never folded into ``discharged``).
``ledger_blocked`` is set when the ledger itself carries a hole that
refuses independent of the results (a ``todo!``/``assume!`` that
emitted no obligation of its own). ``refusals``/``errors`` are the
human-readable reasons the gate reports.
AcceptanceOutcome.title: AcceptanceOutcome

AcceptedDeviation.description:
One row of `dist/acceptance_ledger.json`'s `accepted_deviations`
array (WOG1-F3 below) -- field-for-field what
`regolith.orchestrator.acceptance.acceptance_ledger_bytes` writes.
AcceptedDeviation.title: AcceptedDeviation

ArtifactEntry.description:
One indexed file under a project's `dist/`: its content hash,
a UI-facing relative path (display only, never used to serve),
size, and guessed MIME type.
ArtifactEntry.title: ArtifactEntry

AuditIndex.description: The package-level audit index: total accounting + per-obligation rows.
AuditIndex.title: AuditIndex

AuditRow.description:
One obligation's disposition in the audit index (D221.2).

``detail`` names the exact evidence: for a ``calc_sheet`` the sheet
id; for an ``accepted_deviation`` the waiver target + its memo digest
(cross-linking ``acceptance_ledger.json``, never duplicating it); for
a ``deferred``/``violated`` row the named reason.
AuditRow.title: AuditRow

AuditSummary.description:
The audit index's obligation accounting (D221.2).

TWO honest denominators, both reported so they can never be confused:

* the CENSUS-shape counts (``discharged`` = results with no deferral;
  ``accepted_deviation`` = the number of UNIQUE accepted obligation
  content addresses -- exactly ``len(acceptance.accepted_hashes)``,
  the same value ``fleet._census_from_report`` records; ``violated``)
  so the audit index reconciles with ``fleet_census.json`` field for
  field via :meth:`census_row`;
* the ROW partition (``discharged`` + ``accepted_rows`` + ``deferred``
  + ``violated`` == ``obligations``), because a calc sheet and an
  audit row are PER OBLIGATION and forall-expanded obligation
  instances legitimately share one content address -- so the number
  of accepted ROWS (:attr:`accepted_rows`) can exceed the number of
  unique accepted addresses (:attr:`accepted_deviation`). The row
  partition is the zero-unexplained property (:meth:`balanced`).
AuditSummary.title: AuditSummary

BuildReport.description: The outcome of one orchestrated build at a given tier.
BuildReport.title: BuildReport

BuildTier.description:
The build ladder, ordered by how much work the tier performs.

The integer value IS the order (``IntEnum``): higher tiers include the
lower tiers' work, so ``BuildTier.RELEASE > BuildTier.CHECK`` and
:meth:`includes` are the same fact.
BuildTier.title: BuildTier

CacheStats.description: Hit/miss counters for one orchestration run (observability).
CacheStats.title: CacheStats

CalcInput.description:
One input to a calc sheet's obligation, with its provenance pin.

``value`` is the datum as written (unit-carrying text is kept
verbatim -- the source is the truth, never re-normalized); ``pin`` is
the content address for a ``record_ref`` (else empty). ``source`` is
the human-readable origin (the record name, the literal expression,
or the derived value-source text).
CalcInput.title: CalcInput

ConfigEntry.description:
One `regolith config` key: its effective value and the
where-attribution level that won it (04.1 "ANY FORM/CONFIG FIELD"
floor: source attribution is mandatory, never omitted).
ConfigEntry.title: ConfigEntry

ConfigKeyDefault.description:
One registered key's declared default + doc, for the "reset to
default" affordance (04.1). Neither `regolith config list` nor
`where` prints the default -- only the CURRENT winning value and
its source (WOG1-F5's `--json` gap compounds this: there is no
stable stdout line to parse for "the default" either).

WOG6-F1 (escalation, placeholder): this reads `regolith.config.
registered_keys()` directly (a Python import of the installed
`regolith` wheel), not a CLI subprocess -- the ONE exception to
this module's "always shell out" rule. It is a read of static,
compiled-in registry metadata (key/kind/default/doc), never
project state and never a write, so it does not violate "edits
write through the real CLI" (that doctrine governs mutation, not
read-only introspection of a constant table). Recorded for the
lithos coordinator: a `regolith config list --json` (or a
`--show-default` flag) would let this module drop the import and
go through the CLI uniformly like every other read here.
ConfigKeyDefault.title: ConfigKeyDefault

Coverage.description: Structured coverage (D95, sec. 8.2): per-axis coverage plus the conservative scalar collapse kept for margin notes and old consumers -- `fraction` must never overstate what `axes` states. `Evidence`/`SolverResponse` carry this instead of a bare float.

axes.description: The per-axis coverage record (empty for a closed-form/full claim).
axes.title: Axes

fraction_bits.description: The conservative scalar collapse (`1.0` = full), as `f64` bits.
fraction_bits.title: Fraction Bits

Coverage.title: Coverage

CoverageAxis.description: One axis of structured coverage: the swept variable, its domain, and the method used to cover it (D95, sec. 8.2).

method.description: The method used to cover this axis.
method.title: Method

CoverageAxis.title: CoverageAxis

CoverageDomain2.description: A discrete domain (valve line-ups, failure states, ...).
CoverageDomain2.title: CoverageDomain2

CoverageMethod2.description: A grid with per-axis resolution `k` (multi-dim: `grid(k x m)`, the G29 demand).
CoverageMethod2.title: CoverageMethod2

CoverageMethod4.description: A closed-form analytic bound covers the whole domain.
CoverageMethod4.title: CoverageMethod4

CoverageMethod5.description: Declared monotonicity lets the worst corner stand for the domain.
CoverageMethod5.title: CoverageMethod5

CoverageMethod6.description: WO-33 D98: the axis is DECLARED (a `compute` claim's index domain) but not yet resolved by any model -- the honest pre-discharge state of a [`crate::field::FieldDatum`]'s axis (`payload: null`). Never appears once evidence has landed; a discharging model replaces it with the method it actually used.
CoverageMethod6.title: CoverageMethod6

Deferral.description:
An obligation the orchestrator could not lower to a numeric request.

Honest, greppable, and release-gated: a deferral is neither a pass nor
a violation -- it says "no numeric obligation was formed here" and the
release gate (INV-24) treats it as unresolved.
Deferral.title: Deferral

Deviation.description:
One accepted deviation for the gate summary + acceptance ledger.

Mirrors the source waiver's declared surface (target/scope/basis/
evidence/kind/match set/expiry) plus the obligation content hashes it
actually accepted this build -- the audit row `ship` writes into
``acceptance_ledger.json`` and `ship --explain` lists.
Deviation.title: Deviation

DuplicateModelId.description:
A pack declared a model id something already registered (D-B).

D94: the registry key is ``(claim_kind, model_id)`` -- one model MAY
register under two DIFFERENT kinds legally, so this is a duplicate
only when the SAME kind already has that id. Two models sharing one
id under ONE kind would make selection ambiguous; the whole pack is
skipped (no partial load) and named.
DuplicateModelId.title: DuplicateModelId

DuplicatePluginId.description:
Two plugins of the same kind declared the same id (AD-26: ids are
globally unique per kind; the second is skipped, named by its
source entry point).
DuplicatePluginId.title: DuplicatePluginId

EntryPointRaised.description:
A pack's ``register_fn`` callable raised while composing (not while
the entry point resolved to a manifest -- that failure is a generic
``regolith.plugins.PluginEntryPointRaised``, folded into ``skipped``
the same way). Third-party pack code is a plugin boundary: its
exceptions are OUR recoverable data (the pack is skipped loudly),
never a crashed build.
EntryPointRaised.title: EntryPointRaised

Evidence.description: The evidence produced by discharging one obligation.

coverage.description: Structured per-axis coverage (D95, sec. 8.2); `Coverage::full()` for a closed-form/full claim.

hash.description: Content hash of this evidence (cache key component).
hash.title: Hash

margin_bits.description: The margin after error (`limit - (value + eps)`)'s bits.
margin_bits.title: Margin Bits

model_id.description: The discharge model id that produced this.
model_id.title: Model Id

value_bits.description: The computed value's bits (f64 as bits for exact serialization).
value_bits.title: Value Bits

Evidence.title: Evidence

EvidenceChain.description:
The content-hash chain proving a calc sheet's derivation (D221.1).

``sheet_digest`` is the producer-local blake3 of the sheet's own
canonical bytes (``local-blake3:`` tagged, charter 38 sec. 1.4);
``evidence_hash`` / ``subject_ref`` / ``record_pins`` are the
CANONICAL Rust content addresses off the payload (untagged). A
reviewer follows sheet -> evidence -> payload -> sources without
trusting anything but the shipped bytes.
EvidenceChain.title: EvidenceChain

GateCounts.description:
`dist/gate_summary.json`'s `counts` object verbatim (WOG3-F1
marked-provisional bridge, same posture as `ManifestSummary`/
`AcceptanceLedgerSummary`: no dedicated regolith model for this
on-disk shape exists yet in the public surface).
GateCounts.title: GateCounts

GateSummary.description:
`dist/gate_summary.json` -> the release-gate summary panel (04.1
project-view release-gate deliverable): tier + ok + release_ok +
per-kind counts, read verbatim, never recomputed (charter 3.2).
GateSummary.title: GateSummary

GraphiteSettings.description:
graphite's own preferences: a default project root to land on at
startup, a run verbosity passthrough for driven CLI invocations, and
the run-history retention bound (WOG5-F3). Never a regolith config
key (that precedence ladder is a different doctrine, D163/D164 --
this is graphite-local UI state).
GraphiteSettings.title: GraphiteSettings

HealthSnapshot.description:
A best-effort project-health reading (same two reports
`graphite.server.routes.health.get_project_health` combines), taken
once before a run starts and once after it finishes, so the exit
summary can render a real before -> after verdict diff (deliverable
1) instead of a client-computed one. `None` fields mean the report
was not present/parseable at that moment (e.g. no prior build) --
an honest gap, not a zero.
HealthSnapshot.title: HealthSnapshot

Invalid.description: A present-but-unverifiable attestation: INDETERMINATE, never a verdict.
Invalid.title: Invalid

LockRow.description:
One resolved pin: a slot, its value, and its resolving cause.

Renders as regolith/03 sec. 2's shape::

    flange.radius = 2.4mm         cause: dfm(sheet.min_bend_radius)
LockRow.title: LockRow

LockSection.description: A per-target or per-variant section of rows plus record pins.
LockSection.title: LockSection

Lockfile.description: The full lockfile: tool/registry versions plus ordered sections.
Lockfile.title: Lockfile

MalformedPluginManifest.description: An entry point's target was not a :class:`PluginManifest`.
MalformedPluginManifest.title: MalformedPluginManifest

ManifestSummary.description:
WOG1-F2 marked-provisional bridge: the ship package
`manifest.json` shape trimmed to what the UI needs, pending a real
regolith model (no dedicated one exists in the public surface --
the ship backend assembles this dict ad hoc). Extra keys in the
real file are read but not exposed here; `raw` carries the full
parsed dict for a UI "raw JSON toggle" (04.1 detail-view floor).
ManifestSummary.title: ManifestSummary

MethodNamedKind.description:
A pack declared a claim kind naming a method/tool, not WHAT is
claimed (D94, sec. 8.1: `mech.fea.static_stress` was a bootstrap
error). The whole pack is skipped, naming the offending word.
MethodNamedKind.title: MethodNamedKind

ObligationGroup.description:
One `group=` bucket: the key value (a disposition or a family)
plus its member rows, in source order.
ObligationGroup.title: ObligationGroup

ObligationResult.description:
The outcome of routing one obligation through the harness.

Exactly one of ``evidence`` / ``deferral`` is set: ``evidence`` when a
model (or the total no-model path) produced a verdict, ``deferral``
when the obligation could not even be lowered to a numeric request.
``from_cache`` records whether this was a cache hit (observability).
ObligationResult.title: ObligationResult

ObligationsResponse.description:
The obligations listing: the real `AuditSummary` (unmodified)
plus either a flat `rows` array or, when `group` is given,
`groups` -- never both, so a client always knows which shape to
render.
ObligationsResponse.title: ObligationsResponse

PluginEntryPointRaised.description:
An entry point raised while loading (third-party code is a plugin
boundary: its exceptions are our recoverable data, never a crashed
discovery pass).
PluginEntryPointRaised.title: PluginEntryPointRaised

ProjectHealth.description: The title-block verdict summary for one project.
ProjectHealth.title: ProjectHealth

ProjectInfo.description:
One discovered project: its manifest identity plus which report
artifacts already exist on disk (a fresh project has none of
them -- that is not an error, just an empty-state signal for the
UI's companion-audit empty state, 04.1).
ProjectInfo.title: ProjectInfo

RealizedInput.description:
One caller-resolved realized-domain IR (WO-42 deliverable 3,
AD-25/D128): the orchestrator has already resolved ``digest`` against
the WO-30 content store into ``payload_bytes`` -- this facade does no
IO of its own, only marshals the resolved content across the ONE
coarse FFI crossing (AD-4). ``kind`` is a D96 payload kind (e.g.
``"geometry.realized"``); ``subject`` is the part/block the IR was
realized for (matched against a flownet edge's ``from=`` ref).
RealizedInput.title: RealizedInput

RunRecord.description:
One persisted run: identity, the verb driven, its live/final
status, timestamps, and the project root it ran against. The log
file and (for `--json` verbs) the stdout JSON blob live alongside
it as sibling files (`<id>.log`, `<id>.stdout.json`), not inlined
here -- this record is the cheap-to-list summary row. `before_health`
is captured at `start_run` time (deliverable 1's diff baseline).
RunRecord.title: RunRecord

StagedBuildReport.description:
The outcome of WO-42 deliverable 5's staged build loop (AD-25/D128):
lower -> realize (producing new realized-domain IRs) -> re-lower with
them, to a fixed point.

``final`` is the last :class:`BuildReport` (the fixed-point build --
the one whose ``payload_json`` no longer names a realizable pending
subject). ``iterations`` counts how many core ``check``/``compile``
passes ran (always >= 1: the loop always runs at least once, even
with no ``feature_programs`` supplied). ``realized_inputs`` is every
realized-domain IR supplied to the FINAL pass, and ``lock_rows`` are
their INV-21 lockfile rows (:func:`realized_lock_rows`).
StagedBuildReport.title: StagedBuildReport

Status1.description: The claim holds with margin after the model's error.
Status1.title: Status1

Status2.description: The claim provably fails at its worst corner.
Status2.title: Status2

Status3.description: No adequate model, coverage, or trust; neither proven nor disproven (distinct from violated).
Status3.title: Status3

TrustTier.description:
The total order of evidence trust (regolith/11 sec. 7).

The integer value IS the rank, so ``TrustTier.CERTIFIED > TrustTier.
TESTED`` and floor comparison are the same fact (INV-14 totality).
TrustTier.title: TrustTier

Unsigned.description: No attestation present -- the honest ``community`` tier, not an error.
Unsigned.title: Unsigned

Valid.description: A verified attestation: the evidence earns ``tier`` from a trusted key.
Valid.title: Valid

VerdictDiff.description:
The before/after health-snapshot pair for one run's exit summary
(deliverable 1) -- both sides real reports, never a recomputed
verdict.
VerdictDiff.title: VerdictDiff

get.description:
Every registered key's default/kind/doc, global and static (not
per-project) -- the "reset to default" affordance's data source
since neither `config list` nor `where` prints a default.

schema.title: Response Get Config Schema Api Config Schema Get

get.description:
Liveness probe (not a lithos health report -- see
`/api/projects/{project}/health` for that).

Every project under the server's configured scan root
(04.1 "ANY TABLE" floor: the frontend adds sort/filter/count over
this listing -- the route itself stays a plain array, no
server-side pagination needed at fleet scale).

schema.title: Response List Projects Api Projects Get

get.description: One project's manifest identity + artifact presence flags.

The accepted-deviation ledger (waiver/memo panel, project-view
deliverable 2): every accepted deviation with its memo evidence
digest and basis text, read verbatim (WOG1-F3 provisional bridge).

Every artifact under the project's `dist/` (GLB/SVG/PDF/STEP/
JSON alike), each entry naming its content hash -- the only valid
fetch key (04.1 "ANY GRAPHIC" floor: content-hash caption).

schema.title: Response List Project Artifacts Api Projects  Project  Artifacts Get

get.description:
Raw bytes for one artifact, looked up by content hash ONLY --
`content_hash` must equal an entry this project's own listing
produced; anything else is a 404, never a filesystem read of an
arbitrary path.

The full staged build report, including the BOM/cost/schedule
fields (`cost_profile`, `cost_record_pins`, `cost_estimates`,
`frame_lock_rows`) verbatim from regolith -- no local recomputation
(charter sec. 3.2).

The project's audit index (summary + rows) -- the same model
`/obligations` filters/groups, exposed here unfiltered for a raw
view (04.1 "ANY DETAIL VIEW" floor: raw-JSON toggle).

Every calc sheet in the project's shipped calc book (04.1 "ANY
TABLE" floor: sort/filter/CSV-export are frontend concerns over
this same array, not a server-side feature).

schema.title: Response List Calc Sheets Api Projects  Project  Calc Sheets Get

get.description: Every registered config key with its effective value + source.

schema.title: Response List Project Config Api Projects  Project  Config Get

get.description: One key's effective value + winning source.

put.description:
Write one key through the real `regolith config set` (never a
raw file poke).

get.description:
Every registered optional external tool's found/missing status
(regolith's own report -- graphite renders it verbatim, per the
read-mostly honesty posture).

schema.title: Response Get Doctor Api Projects  Project  Doctor Get

get.description:
The release-gate summary panel (WOG3-F1, provisional bridge --
project-view deliverable 2).

`release_ok` from the build report + the audit index's obligation
accounting -- both read verbatim, never recomputed.
The resolved `regolith.lock` (every pinned slot + its cause).
The ship package manifest summary (WOG1-F2, provisional bridge).

The project's obligation rows, optionally filtered by disposition
and/or grouped by disposition or claim family.
This project's run history, newest first.

schema.title: Response List Project Runs Api Projects  Project  Runs Get

post.description:
Start `regolith <verb> <args>` against `project`'s root; returns
immediately with a `running` record (04.1 "ANY LONG OPERATION"
floor: a durable record + elapsed time via `started_at` for the
frontend to compute).

get.description:
One run's current record (re-polls liveness when still `running`,
see `graphite.service.runs.get_run`).

post.description:
Stop a running run (deliverable 1's cancel affordance; WOG1-F6
closed -- no kill route existed before WO-G5).

get.description:
SSE stream of `run_id`'s log lines + parsed progress events,
closing with a `done` event once the run finishes.

The full captured log for `run_id` as a plain array -- run-
history detail replay (deliverable 2), no SSE subscription needed
for a run that has already finished (or is still running; this
still returns whatever has been captured so far).

schema.title: Response Get Run Log Api Runs  Run Id  Log Get

get.description:
The before/after health-snapshot pair for `run_id`'s exit summary
(deliverable 1) -- both real report reads, never a recomputed
verdict (charter sec. 3.2).
The current graphite settings (recorded defaults on first run).

post.description: Reset graphite's own settings to their recorded defaults.
